blocks.content: Shirone sensed he couldn't stay here long and asked his second question.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How can one learn magic? Does it require a special power?
It was an unexpectedly mundane answer.
Shirone thought Alpheas might be hiding the truth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that really all? Do you mean if I think about creating wind, I could fly like you just did?
That's a rather difficult question.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But to exaggerate somewhat, yes.
Of course, mere thinking isn't enough.
A magician's mind must perfectly harmonize with the world.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: It's like an extremely sensitive mental state.
The children, seeing their principal seriously conversing with someone for the first time, didn't dare interrupt.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How does one experience this extremely sensitive mental state?
Alpheas understood Shirone's intentions and smiled slightly.
This wasn't mere curiosity. This child was exploring.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: When a magician concentrates, his mind becomes so sensitive that he can sense everything around him.
Magicians call this entering the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I'll show you a demonstration. Let one of these children show you.
The girl named Shuamin answered confidently.

blocks.speaker: Shuamin
blocks.content: Yes, Principal.
As Shuamin closed her eyes to concentrate, the children created a quiet atmosphere.

blocks.speaker: Shuamin
blocks.content: I've entered the Spirit Zone.
Alpheas took out a coin and shook it.
Then he grabbed the coin and extended his fist.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Now, how many coins are in here?

blocks.speaker: Shuamin
blocks.content: Six.
When he opened his palm, six silver coins lay there.
Ignoring Shirone's surprised expression, Alpheas repeated the action.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Let's try again.

blocks.speaker: Shuamin
blocks.content: Three.
She was correct again.
He tried several more times with the same result.
Alpheas put away the coins with a satisfied expression.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: That's enough, Shuamin. Well done.
Shuamin exhaled deeply.
Even though she'd only guessed numbers, beads of cold sweat had formed on her forehead.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: This is what an extremely sensitive mental state means.
Upon entering the Spirit Zone, a magician can perceive the external world with heightened senses.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Outstanding magicians can even count leaves on distant trees.
Of course, what Shuamin demonstrated is impressive too.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I felt he understood. I too had achieved the Thunder Strike by intuiting an impossible-to-find weak point through insight.
By his reasoning, Shuamin hadn't counted the coins.
She had concentrated all her senses and perceived the entire situation inside Alpheas's hand.
Anyone can do it.
blocks.speaker: Shirone

blocks.content: Alpheas said, reading Shirone's thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Anyone can enter the Spirit Zone.
But not just anyone can do it.
Innate talent.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: That was the world of magic.
Practice in a quiet place.
blocks.speaker: Alpheas
Alpheas
blocks.content: First, feel yourself. Then erase yourself.
If you can do that, a different world will unfold before you.
blocks.speaker: Alpheas
Shirone
blocks.content: I understand.
Shirone got it.
If one cannot feel oneself, then one cannot erase oneself either.
blocks.speaker: Shirone

blocks.content: Alpheas was impressed again.
This wasn't an insight a 12-year-old could normally comprehend.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Would you try it? Here and now?
Seeing no reason to refuse, Shirone closed his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He thought he might succeed by drawing on his Thunder Strike experience.
But once he concentrated, he realized this was different from finding a tree's weak point.
He couldn't visualize anything.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Trying to recall his own appearance only produced images constructed mostly from visual information.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was shocking how little he knew about himself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What am I? Who is this being called myself?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone delved endlessly into his thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Finally, he reached a thought about himself thinking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The brain. Humans are their brains.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter what he recalled, he couldn't clearly define himself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Humans are merely brain functions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If thinking is all I am, then when I stop thinking, I cease to exist.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone began gradually erasing his brain functions.
It was something anyone could do, but not just anyone could accomplish.
His mind felt like it was being sucked into an abyss.
For the first time, he felt he could see the form of concentration.
As his nervous system connected to his five senses dissolved, he could no longer distinguish what was smell and what was touch.
And finally, Shirone disappeared from his own brain.
Shirone's eyes snapped open.
The scene before him was peaceful.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: How was it? Did you hear anything?
Alpheas wasn't expecting much.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I heard.
Surprised by the unexpected answer, Alpheas raised his eyebrows.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Oh? What did you hear?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sounds. I heard all sounds.
Well now, I see.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Alpheas nodded.
Shirone probably hadn't entered the Spirit Zone.
Even an ordinary person can hear all sorts of things after ten minutes of concentration.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But what happens in the Spirit Zone is on an entirely different level.
First, one experiences synesthesia.
Sounds emit scents, light produces tastes, and images are felt through the skin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It seems impossible right away after all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What a shame. A child with potential.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he had been born a noble, if he had trained from childhood, might he have achieved similar accomplishments as these children here?
But that alone wasn't enough to change Alpheas's mind.
The world is full of talented people, as numerous as stars.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: He couldn't even guarantee that all the children present would become magicians.
Well done.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: With diligent practice, you'll be able to hear even more sounds.
Alpheas patted Shirone and walked away with the children.
Shirone knew he was disappointed.
But even the fact that he didn't check to see if Shirone had left the school was kindness enough.
Shirone climbed over the wall and ran frantically.
Only after stopping and sitting down did he notice his heart pounding against his ribcage.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It really worked.
Γùå Γùå Γùå
Shirone heard all sounds.
Of course, there were other sensations too, but he couldn't tell Alpheas about them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When his vague ideal became tangible, all his thoughts warned him that proceeding further would be dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps he hadn't entered the Spirit Zone after all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He still couldn't define what it was.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It felt as if his body was breaking into particles and dispersing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As his senses opened, all kinds of information poured in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He trembled at the vastness of a world entirely different from what he perceived with his five senses.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Simultaneously, one truth pierced his mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I am infinite.
Hic, sob.
Shirone shed tears from overwhelming emotion.
He closed his eyes tightly and ran.
He didn't even realize he was running.
His awakened brain secreted an enormous amount of adrenaline, causing him to temporarily black out.
Oof!
Shirone clutched his nose and fell on his backside.
Coming to his senses, he found himself in an alley.
The gloomy place Vincent had strictly forbidden him to enter.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: What the hell, how annoying!
The young man who had been hit by Shirone's back fell and snapped angrily.
In front of him stood some rough-looking men glaring at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: What's your problem? Are you crazy?
He had slanted eyes and a viciously twisted lip.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry!
The ruffians smiled cruelly at Shirone's terrified eyes.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: You think saying sorry makes it okay? Tell me the truth. Did the Wolf gang send you? Were you trying to ambush me just now?
No! No!
blocks.speaker: Shirone

blocks.content: The ruffians smiled cruelly at Shirone's terrified eyes.
This kid, who looked cute even when fallen, didn't have the courage to hit anyone, let alone wield a knife.
In the back alleys, they called such types 'herbivores.'
And herbivores were good sources of income for carnivores.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh? Look at this.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Judging by his face, though poorly dressed, he had noble features.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A child like this could be sold to the South Village for at least one gold coin as premium labor.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: What are you guys doing? Did something fun happen?
Lady Eimy!
The ruffians turned around in unison.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Hehe. What brings you here? Bored again?
Despite his casual tone, the young man rubbed his hands obsequiously.
Anyone who knew her identity would do the same.
Karmis Eimy.
The youngest daughter of the House of Karmis, a first-rank noble family.
Born with everything, she had grown tired of life and lately found her only joy in prowling back alleys looking for entertainment.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: This bastard here, miss, tried to kill someone. So we were just about to teach him a lesson.
Shirone shook his head urgently.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, no. I bumped into him by accident.
You're lying. You expect us to believe that?
A burly man punched Shirone in the stomach, sending him rolling on the ground without even a chance to scream.
His breath stopped, and his stomach felt like it was being torn apart.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was the first violence he had ever experienced.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: You have to pay compensation, punk! Where's your mother? Bring that mother who gave birth to a conscienceless bastard like you, probably stuffing her face with bread somewhere!
Shirone was shocked.
How could they speak like that when they had mothers too?
They truly were bad people.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: What's this? Your eyes are changing? Are you upset?
As Shirone's expression hardened, the ruffians jumped in and started stomping on him.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Stop. You'll kill him at this rate.
At Eimy's command, the ruffians backed off.
Shirone looked at her with frightened eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He envied how free she seemed from pain, as if there were an invisible glass wall between them.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Hey! Are you okay?
I, I didn't do anything.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Who said you did? I asked if you're okay.
I don't know if I am.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone wanted to go home.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps this girl would let him leave.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: What are you going to do now? Anything more entertaining?
Shirone looked at Eimy with eyes full of betrayal and fear.
But she just grinned mischievously.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: We were thinking of relieving him of his valuables and then selling him to a male brothel.
Shirone's face turned as pale as thin soup.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Seeing the frightened boy's face, even Eimy felt a little sorry for him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But she had no intention of playing with these back alley trash in the same way.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She planned to scare him a little and then send him home.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: For a brothel, he must be flawless. How about we strip him and take a look?
Eimy made a bold suggestion.
Nobles matured earlier than commoners, so at 12 years old, she was quite knowledgeable in theory.
Her curiosity was strong, and she'd been wanting to see for herself.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Besides... he's handsome, isn't he? To be honest.
She wouldn't want to see a rough adult's naked body even if paid.
Fortunately, Shirone was of similar age and had an appearance she liked.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Take your clothes off. Who knows? If you do what you're told diligently, I might send you home.
Shirone went blank while receiving numerous gazes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He couldn't understand why he deserved such treatment.

blocks.speaker: Young Man
blocks.content: Hey, the young lady told you to strip! Want another beating?
The ruffian barked, but Shirone didn't even react.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Eimy felt slightly uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: While a 12-year-old noble might be considered grown up, a commoner was still a child.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: He might have lost his mind from fear beyond his limits.
But Shirone wasn't insane.
He was more focused than ever.
Without realizing it, he had entered the Spirit Zone in his panic state.
This second experience of the Spirit Zone was different from the first.
All kinds of information swirled around him.
He could even hear the ruffians blinking.
In this hypersensitive mental world, Shirone let his consciousness flow along the only path available.
At the destination of this path lay the memory of Alpheas casting magic.
Shirone's intuition gathered all the surface information of Alpheas casting magic—emotions, sensations, mental state, actions, attitudes, posture.
As the external form took shape, the internal intent became predictable, and a concept suddenly appeared in his mind.
You bastard! Aren't you going to pull down your pants? The young lady wants to see!
The Spirit Zone, requiring high concentration, is vulnerable to physical shock.
Shirone almost fell out of it when the ruffian grabbed his collar and shook him.
As an eerie energy rose in Shirone's pupils, Eimy reached out and shouted:

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Wait a moment!
But the situation was already irreversible.
Shirone's mind, precariously balanced between reality and the Spirit Zone, recognized the danger and instinctively twisted phenomena.
Whoosh!
High-density wind surged, lifting everything in the alley.
Not only the ruffians but also Eimy went flying.
Like a typhoon had swept through, not a wooden box, stone, or speck of dust remained.
Aaaah! Help!
Hearing screams from the sky, Shirone snapped out of the Zone.
Coming to his senses, he saw an empty, desolate alley.
Soon after, people who had risen higher than the rooftops began falling with terrifying screams.
Thud! Thud! Thud! Thud! Thud!
Heavy sounds that foreshadowed impact hit the ground.
Gravity being a force much stronger than common sense suggested, the ruffians' limbs broke upon landing.
Urgh...
My, my arm. My leg...
Legs were bent grotesquely, and arm bones protruded through skin by a handspan.
It looked like discarded wooden puppets strewn about carelessly.
Shirone was speechless, never having expected a moment's emotion to cause such a devastating result.
At that moment, one person landed on the ground intact—Eimy.
Nobles train their bodies systematically from childhood.
Especially those of the first rank.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: You... what exactly are you?
The scene Shirone created vividly demonstrated why magicians were feared.
Even she, with her innate athletic abilities, couldn't resist the sudden updraft.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Answer me! Where did you learn magic? How can a commoner...!
Eimy stopped mid-sentence and looked back.
People were gathering, drawn by the screams.
If rumors spread that she had been associating with ruffians, her family would be in an uproar.

blocks.speaker: Eimy
blocks.content: Tch!
Eimy placed one foot against a building.
Kicking off the wall, she flew up, pushed off the wall surface again with both feet, and disappeared onto the roof of the opposite building.
Huh? Huh?
Shirone, who had been trying to follow her zigzagging movement, didn't know what to do when she disappeared.
If people saw this scene, he would be blamed for everything.
The city is cruel to the powerless; no one would take his side.
Shirone fled in the opposite direction.
He moved his legs frantically, seeking his father, the only one he could trust and rely on.
